body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Service-channel authorization goes stale after user switches.**

`active_uid_cached()` can already lag by up to a second, and `start()` only consults it once when the stream is accepted. The background config-sync loop keeps `_service` open and reuses that connection for later `SyncConfig` writes, so a process that was authorized while user A was active can keep writing config after user B becomes active. Revalidate protected streams against the current active UID before handling each message, or tear them down when the active user changes.



Also applies to: 856-864, 893-913

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/ipc.rs` around lines 121 - 153, The authorization for long-lived
protected streams is stale because start() only checks active_uid_cached() once;
change the stream handling so that before processing each incoming message (or
before performing each SyncConfig write via _service) you re-check the current
active UID (call active_uid_cached() or active_uid_strict() for a fresh check)
and compare it to the UID that was used when the stream was accepted, and if
they differ immediately close/tear down the stream and reject the message;
alternatively, add a watcher that invalidates/clears the cached UID (or forces
stream termination) when active_uid changes so protected streams cannot continue
using previous authorization.
```

</details>

body: The PR description states the protected `_service` channel is “SyncConfig-only”, but the allowlist enforcement is currently compiled only for Linux/macOS. Either (mandatory) update the PR description to make this Unix-only, or (preferred if the intent is cross-platform) implement an equivalent allowlist on Windows and move any non-SyncConfig traffic (e.g., `UserSid`) to a different dedicated channel to keep `_service` truly SyncConfig-only everywhere.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This log message says “protected ipc_service channel”, but the helper is invoked for any `is_service_ipc_postfix` (i.e., potentially more than just `_service`). Consider rewording to something like “service-scoped IPC channel” or guard usage so this function is only used when `postfix == POSTFIX_SERVICE`, to avoid misleading operational/security logs.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The SO_PEERCRED + “root or active uid” policy is duplicated here and in `src/ipc.rs` (`peer_uid()` / `is_allowed_service_peer_uid()` / `service_authorization_status()`). To reduce drift in security policy (and keep logging/active-uid caching consistent), consider extracting a shared helper for “peer uid + allow policy” in `crate::ipc` and reusing it from uinput (or reusing `is_allowed_service_peer_uid` at minimum).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: When `cm_uid` is set, failures to connect via `connect_for_uid()` unconditionally `continue`, so the legacy/default `_cm` path is never attempted. This can cause upgrade/rollback interop issues if the spawned CM process (or a still-running older version) is using the prior non-per-UID socket layout. Consider trying `connect_for_uid()` first and then falling back to `connect()` (optionally with a deprecation log) to make mixed-version transitions more robust.

Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/server/uinput.rs | Adds Linux uinput IPC peer authorization hook (UID-based). | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds Linux `connect_for_uid` path for CM IPC in a specific headless run-as-user flow. | | src/server.rs | Switches `_service` IPC connection call sites to `connect_service()`. | | src/platform/windows.rs | Adds Windows service-side IPC authorization (session-consistent) and a helper to detect SYSTEM peers with tests. | | src/platform/linux.rs | Improves error handling/logging for terminal session count lookup. | | src/ipc.rs | Major IPC hardening: secure parent dir creation/validation, platform-specific authorization, throttled rejection logs, `_service` allowlist, connect helpers, and expanded tests. | | src/common.rs | Removes an unused `is_installed` import. |

body: `new_listener` now sets non-service IPC socket permissions to `0600`. The Linux root service starts the uinput IPC servers (`_uinput_keyboard/_uinput_mouse/_uinput_control`), but the per-user `--server` process connects via `ipc::connect(...)` (see `setup_uinput()`), so a root-owned `0600` socket will prevent the user process from connecting (regression vs previous `0777`). Either ensure `_uinput_*` postfixes are treated as service-scoped (world-connectable + accept-time UID authorization), or special-case uinput sockets here to remain connectable by the active user while still enforcing authorization.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `authorize_uinput_peer()` currently skips authorization unless `is_service_ipc_postfix(postfix)` is true. Given the uinput servers listen on `_uinput_*` postfixes, this can easily become a no-op, leaving the uinput IPC channels either inaccessible (if sockets remain `0600`) or unauthenticated (if permissions are relaxed elsewhere). Consider making uinput authorization unconditional for these uinput postfixes (or ensure the service-postfix predicate explicitly includes them) so the intended UID-based gating is always applied.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/server/uinput.rs | Adds Linux-only peer authorization for uinput IPC connections using peer UID and active UID checks. | | src/server/connection.rs | Attempts to reconnect to CM via uid-scoped IPC path when CM is spawned as a specific user. | | src/server.rs | Switches service IPC reconnects to the new `connect_service()` helper. | | src/platform/windows.rs | Adds Windows service-side IPC authorization tied to the currently served session; introduces `is_process_running_as_system()` + tests. | | src/platform/linux.rs | Improves error handling/logging when terminal session count IPC is unavailable. | | src/ipc.rs | Major IPC hardening: secure parent dir, stricter permissions, accept-time authorization, `_service` allowlist, uid-scoped connect helpers, terminal session count improvements, and tests. | | src/common.rs | Removes an unused `is_installed` import. |

body: On Linux, `cm_uid` is only derived when `user` is `Some(...)` (headless branch), but the root path also spawns CM via `platform::run_as_user(..., user=None, ...)`, which still runs CM as the active non-root user. In that common case `cm_uid` stays `None` and the reconnect loop falls back to `ipc::connect(..., "_cm")`, which targets the root (euid 0) socket path and can’t reach the user CM socket if IPC paths are uid-scoped. Consider always determining the uid of the user you spawned CM as (even when `user` is `None`) and using `connect_for_uid` for that uid (or trying both active uid and 0 similarly to `get_terminal_session_count()`).

## Pull request overview This PR tightens IPC security and correctness across platforms by hardening Unix IPC filesystem handling, adding accept-time peer authorization (Linux/macOS/Windows), and fixing a Linux root→user IPC connection path regression. **Changes:** - Harden Unix IPC listener setup (secure parent directory handling, restrictive socket/pid permissions) and add peer/message authorization for protected channels. - Add Linux uinput IPC peer authorization and improve Linux `_cm` connection logic by connecting to the target user’s IPC path when appropriate. - Add Windows IPC authorization improvements plus a helper to detect whether an arbitrary process is running as LocalSystem (with tests). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/server/uinput.rs | Authorize Linux uinput IPC peers for service-scoped postfixes before accepting connections. | | src/server/connection.rs | On Linux, connect to `_cm` using the spawned user’s IPC path (uid-scoped) to avoid cross-user path mismatches. | | src/server.rs | Use the new `ipc::connect_service()` helper for `_service` connections. | | src/platform/windows.rs | Recompute/validate expected session for service IPC authorization; add `is_process_running_as_system()` + tests. | | src/platform/linux.rs | Make terminal session count failures non-fatal with a debug log and safe fallback. | | src/ipc.rs | Core IPC hardening: secure parent dir, socket/pid perms, accept-time authorization, `_service` message allowlist, uid-scoped connect helper, throttled rejection logging, tests. | | src/common.rs | Remove unused `is_installed` import. |

## Pull request overview This PR hardens and standardizes IPC behavior across platforms, with a focus on protecting service-scoped channels and fixing Linux cross-user IPC connection behavior. **Changes:** - Added accept-time peer authorization and throttled logging for protected IPC channels (Unix UID-based; Windows session/SYSTEM-based). - Hardened Unix IPC socket parent directory creation/validation (O_NOFOLLOW + fstat/fchmod/fchown) and tightened socket/pidfile permissions. - Fixed a Linux regression when root spawns the connection manager as another user by connecting to the correct per-UID IPC socket. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/server/uinput.rs | Adds Linux peer authorization for protected uinput IPC connections and minor log formatting. | | src/server/connection.rs | On Linux, connects to `_cm` using the spawned CM user’s UID to target the correct socket path. | | src/server.rs | Uses new `connect_service()` helper for the protected `_service` IPC channel. | | src/platform/windows.rs | Enforces session-consistent IPC authorization for service connections; adds process LocalSystem detection + tests. | | src/platform/linux.rs | Improves robustness by logging and defaulting when terminal session count cannot be queried. | | src/ipc.rs | Implements IPC security hardening (dir validation, socket perms), adds `connect_service()`/`connect_for_uid()`, and introduces authorization + throttled rejection logs. | | src/common.rs | Removes an unused `is_installed` import from `ui_interface`. |

## Pull request overview This PR tightens IPC security across platforms (Linux/macOS/Windows) by adding peer authorization, hardening IPC filesystem setup, and fixing a Linux cross-user IPC connection regression (root spawning a user CM but connecting to the wrong per-uid socket path). **Changes:** - Add IPC peer authorization + rejection logging/throttling, plus a strict message allowlist on the protected `_service` channel. - Harden Unix IPC socket parent directories and restrict Unix socket permissions (0600 by default; 0666 only for service-scoped channels where accept-time auth is enforced). - Fix Linux root→user Connection Manager IPC by connecting to the user-specific `_cm` socket path; add Windows service-side session-aware authorization checks. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/server/uinput.rs | Adds Linux uinput IPC peer authorization before accepting connections. | | src/server/connection.rs | Connects to `_cm` via per-uid IPC path on Linux when CM is launched as a user. | | src/server.rs | Uses the new `connect_service()` helper for `_service` connections. | | src/platform/windows.rs | Adds session-consistent IPC authorization for service connections; adds `is_process_running_as_system()` + tests. | | src/platform/linux.rs | Improves error handling/logging when terminal session count IPC is unavailable. | | src/ipc.rs | Core IPC hardening: secure parent dir setup, peer UID/PID auth helpers, allowlist for `_service`, permission tightening, connect helpers, and tests. | | src/common.rs | Removes an unused `is_installed` import. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: On Windows, `AsRawHandle::as_raw_handle()` returns a `RawHandle` (pointer). Constructing `windows::Win32::Foundation::HANDLE` as `HANDLE(pipe_handle)` is very likely a type mismatch and won’t compile (HANDLE expects an integer-sized handle). Consider explicitly casting the raw handle to the expected `HANDLE` representation (or using the correct handle wrapper/type expected by `GetNamedPipeClientProcessId`).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Pull request overview This PR tightens IPC security/authorization across platforms and fixes IPC connection pathing issues on Linux when processes run under different UIDs (e.g., root spawning user-scoped components). **Changes:** - Add peer authorization + throttled logging for protected/service-scoped IPC channels (Linux/macOS UID-based; Windows session/SYSTEM-based), plus a strict message allowlist for `_service` on Unix. - Harden Unix IPC socket parent directory handling and reduce overly-permissive socket/pidfile permissions. - Fix/extend Linux IPC connectivity to support connecting to a specific user’s `_cm` socket, and update callers to use the new helpers. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ipc.rs | Core IPC hardening: secure parent dir, socket perms, peer authorization helpers, throttled rejection logs, connect helpers, and new tests | | src/server/connection.rs | Linux `_cm` connection now supports uid-specific socket paths (connect_for_uid) | | src/server/uinput.rs | Adds Linux uinput IPC peer authorization using fd-based peer UID | | src/server.rs | Switch config sync to `connect_service()` helper for `_service` | | src/platform/windows.rs | Enforces service IPC authorization consistency and adds SYSTEM detection for arbitrary processes (+ tests) | | src/platform/linux.rs | Improves handling/logging when terminal session count IPC query fails | | src/common.rs | Removes unused `is_installed` import |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `cm_uid` is only used in the post-spawn connection retry loop. If `_cm` is already running under the target user UID, the earlier initial `connect("_cm")` attempt (which uses the current effective UID path) will still fail and we’ll unnecessarily spawn a second CM process. Consider attempting `connect_for_uid(..., cm_uid, "_cm")` immediately after computing `cm_uid` (before spawning) and only spawning CM if that connect fails.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview This PR hardens IPC on desktop platforms by adding peer authorization (UID/session-based), tightening Unix socket permissions, and fixing Linux cross-UID IPC connection behavior (e.g., root spawning a user CM but still trying to connect via uid 0 paths). **Changes:** - Add accept-time IPC authorization and throttled logging for unauthorized peers (Windows session/SYSTEM checks; Linux/macOS UID checks) plus a SyncConfig-only allowlist for the protected `_service` channel. - Introduce Linux `connect_for_uid()` and update CM connection logic to connect to the correct user’s IPC socket when CM is launched as that user. - Tighten Unix IPC socket/pidfile permissions and harden IPC parent directory handling (anti-symlink/TOCTOU). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/server/uinput.rs | Adds Linux uinput IPC peer authorization based on peer UID for service-scoped postfixes. | | src/server/connection.rs | Connects to `_cm` using `connect_for_uid()` when CM is started as a specific Linux user. | | src/server.rs | Switches `_service` connections to the new `connect_service()` helper. | | src/platform/windows.rs | Adds Windows-side session-consistent service IPC authorization and implements `is_process_running_as_system()` with tests. | | src/platform/linux.rs | Improves error handling/logging when terminal session count cannot be queried. | | src/ipc.rs | Core IPC security changes: secure parent dir handling, per-platform authorization, `_service` allowlist, permission tightening, `connect_service()`/`connect_for_uid()`, and new tests. | | src/common.rs | Removes an unused `is_installed` import. |

body: On Windows, `AsRawHandle::as_raw_handle()` returns a `RawHandle` (a pointer). `windows::Win32::Foundation::HANDLE` is typically a newtype over an integer-sized handle value, so `HANDLE(pipe_handle)` is likely a type mismatch and/or the wrong conversion. Convert the raw handle to the expected HANDLE representation (e.g., cast to the underlying integer type) before calling `GetNamedPipeClientProcessId`, and consider treating `INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE` as invalid in addition to null.

## Pull request overview This PR strengthens IPC security and correctness across platforms, including protected `_service` authorization, Linux UID-specific IPC connections (fixing cross-user regressions), and Windows session/system peer validation. **Changes:** - Adds accept-time IPC peer authorization + throttled logging, plus a stricter `_service` message surface (SyncConfig allowlist) on Linux/macOS. - Introduces `connect_service()` and Linux `connect_for_uid()` and updates call sites to use the appropriate connector. - Improves Windows IPC authorization consistency and adds a robust “process is LocalSystem” check with tests; also hardens Linux server restart logic around terminal session count retrieval. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/ipc.rs` | Core IPC hardening: secure parent dir handling, per-platform peer authorization helpers, `_service` allowlist, new connect helpers, and new tests. | | `src/server/uinput.rs` | Adds Linux-only accept-time authorization for uinput IPC peers on service-scoped postfixes. | | `src/server/connection.rs` | Fixes Linux root→user CM connection by connecting to the spawned user’s UID-specific `_cm` socket path. | | `src/server.rs` | Switches `_service` connects to `connect_service()` for consistency. | | `src/platform/windows.rs` | Adds Windows service-side IPC authorization tied to the served session; introduces `is_process_running_as_system()` and tests. | | `src/platform/linux.rs` | Makes terminal session count failures explicit (debug log) and defaults to 0 to preserve prior behavior. | | `src/common.rs` | Removes an unused import (`is_installed`). |

body: The protected `_service` allowlist only rejects well-formed `Data` variants that aren’t `SyncConfig`. If a peer sends non-JSON / non-UTF8 frames, `ConnectionTmpl::next()` returns `Ok(None)` and this branch is skipped, leaving the connection open despite receiving invalid traffic (contradicting the intent described in the comments). Consider treating `Ok(None)` as invalid for `_service` (log + close) to fully enforce the allowlist and avoid keeping a protected channel alive under garbage input.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Resolve CM uid before selecting per-uid IPC path**

On Linux, `run_as_user(..., user=None, ...)` launches `--cm` as the active desktop user (see `platform::linux::run_as_user`), but this block leaves `cm_uid` as `None` unless headless mode explicitly populated `user`. In root-service flows where `start_ipc` runs outside the headless branch, the retry loop then falls back to `connect(..., "_cm")`, which targets the root uid socket path under the new per-uid IPC layout, so connecting to the newly launched CM can fail consistently.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
author: chatgpt-codex-connector

body: On Linux, `--server` runs as a user process, and `--cm` does the same.
author: fufesou

body:
## Pull request overview This PR hardens permanent (“master”) password handling by eliminating reversible storage/exposure and switching to a non-reversible verifier, while keeping legacy compatibility and preventing UI/IPC leakage. **Changes:** - Return only “is set” state (`""`/empty) over UI/IPC and remove UI prefill of the permanent password. - Update server-side password validation to accept `01$...` hashed verifier storage as well as legacy plaintext-like storage. - Update Flutter UI flows to avoid reading/prefilling secrets and allow clearing the permanent password. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Mobile-side `permanent_password()` now returns only “is set” state instead of secret material. | | src/ui/index.tis | Removes pre-filling password/confirmation fields in the Sciter UI dialog. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds support for `01$` verifier format during auth validation. | | src/ipc.rs | Ensures IPC does not expose secret material and adjusts setter/getter behavior accordingly. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Updates preset-password check to work with verifier storage by recomputing and comparing H1. | | libs/hbb_common | Bumps submodule to bring in config/verifier-related changes. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Changes success detection to “is set” semantics rather than secret readback. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Stops prefill; enables clearing password; updates validation/submit gating. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Stops prefill and resets reactive state accordingly. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Fail-open fallback from an invalid `01$...` verifier to treating the entire string as plaintext changes authentication semantics and can unintentionally allow authentication with the literal (corrupted/tampered) stored value. For verifier-formatted values, it’s safer to fail closed (return `false`) when base64 decoding fails or length != 32, and log at `warn` (or once) to avoid repeated noisy errors.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `get_permanent_password()` no longer returns a password; it returns a sentinel (`\"<set>\"`) or empty. Keeping the existing name makes accidental misuse more likely (e.g., callers treating the return value as secret material, hashing it, comparing it, etc.). Consider introducing/renaming to a dedicated API like `has_permanent_password()` / `get_permanent_password_state()` (boolean or enum) and keeping `get_permanent_password()` reserved for actual secret access (if any platform still needs it).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Similar to IPC: `permanent_password()` now returns a sentinel/empty rather than the password on mobile builds. The function name strongly suggests it returns actual secret material, which increases the risk of future call sites mishandling `\"<set>\"` as a password. Consider renaming this surface to reflect the new contract (e.g., `permanent_password_state()` / `has_permanent_password()`), or at minimum add a doc comment stating the return value is not the password and is intended only for UI state.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The plaintext-to-H1 hashing logic is duplicated in both the verifier fallback branch and the non-verifier branch. Extracting it into a small helper (e.g., `compute_h1_from_plain(password, salt)`) reduces duplication and makes it easier to keep hashing behavior consistent across branches.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Pull request overview This PR aims to prevent RustDesk’s Linux/X11 path from crashing when the active window disappears between querying the active window and querying its geometry (X11 `BadWindow`), by installing a temporary custom X11 error handler around the libxdo window-geometry calls. **Changes:** - Adds FFI bindings for `XSetErrorHandler`/`XSync` and an `XErrorEvent` struct, plus a custom X11 error handler that suppresses `BadWindow`. - Wraps `xdo_get_window_location`/`xdo_get_window_size` in `get_focused_display()` with handler install/restore, and attempts to flush X errors before restoring. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: XSetErrorHandler is process-global, so swapping the handler inside get_focused_display can race with other threads that also use X11/libxdo (and can also cause this function to observe a BadWindow that occurred in a different thread via the global X_BAD_WINDOW_DETECTED). Consider protecting set/restore with a global mutex + using a thread-local flag for detection, or installing a single global handler once and scoping detection per-thread/call via thread-local state.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: XSync is called on the thread-local DISPLAY opened via XOpenDisplay(), but the libxdo context created with xdo_new(NULL) typically owns a different Display connection. If the BadWindow error is queued on xdo's Display, this XSync may not force delivery before you restore the previous error handler, so the process can still hit the default fatal handler later. To make the flush effective, sync the same Display used by libxdo (e.g., construct XDO via xdo_new_with_opened_display using this DISPLAY, or otherwise obtain/sync xdo's Display).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`In Xlib, is XSetErrorHandler process-global or scoped per Display/thread? What are the concurrency guarantees if multiple threads temporarily replace the handler around X11 calls?`

💡 Result:

`XSetErrorHandler()` installs **one error handler per process (global)** — it is **not scoped per `Display*`** and **not thread-local**. This follows from Xlib’s own API/wording: there is a single “program’s supplied error handler”, set/resettable “as often as you like”, and the function does **not** take a `Display*` argument. The call returns the *previous* handler, i.e., it replaces a single global function pointer. [1][2]

### Concurrency / “temporarily replace around a call” in multi-threaded programs
Even if you call `XInitThreads()` (enabling Xlib’s internal locking) and/or use `XLockDisplay()`/`XUnlockDisplay()` (which serialize Xlib use **for a given `Display*`**), **there is no safe guarantee** that “replace handler → do X11 call → restore handler” will only affect errors from that call/thread, because:

- X protocol errors are often delivered **later** (Xlib buffers requests; errors can surface on `XSync()`, on later round-trips, etc.). So the error for a request you made while handler=A can be dispatched after you already restored handler=B (or while another thread has installed handler=C). [3][4]
- `XLockDisplay()` only blocks other threads from using *that display*; it does **not** scope the global error handler to that display/thread. Another thread using a different `Display*` (or even the same one, once unlocked) can race with your handler changes. [4]
- Therefore, “temporary replacement” is inherently racy unless you provide **your own process-wide synchronization** that prevents *any* concurrent Xlib/X11 activity that could trigger error delivery while the handler is swapped—and you typically also need to force synchronization (`XSync`) before restoring to ensure any pending errors have been processed under the intended handler. [3][4]

**Practical implication:** if multiple threads might touch Xlib, don’t rely on per-thread/per-call handler swapping. Treat the Xlib error handler as **process-global state** and protect any changes with a **global mutex**, and be aware that even then, asynchronous error delivery can defeat “scoped” intent unless you also add the necessary round-trips (e.g., `XSync`) before restoring. For robust per-request error handling, many projects prefer XCB’s “checked” request style rather than Xlib’s global callback model. [3]

**Sources:** Xlib reference for `XSetErrorHandler()` [1], XInitThreads/XLockDisplay man page [4], Xlib spec discussion of asynchronous error reporting [3], plus corroborating discussion of per-process handler and multi-thread pitfalls [2].

---



</details>

**Guard the temporary `XSetErrorHandler` swap with a process-wide mutex.**

`XSetErrorHandler` changes **process-global** Xlib state—not scoped per `Display` or thread. Without a lock around the full set/call/sync/restore window, concurrent X11 activity can race with the handler swap, observe the temporary handler, flip the shared `X_BAD_WINDOW_DETECTED` flag, or restore a stale previous handler.

Additionally, Xlib buffers X protocol requests and delivers errors asynchronously. An error triggered by a request made while the temporary handler is installed may not be delivered until *after* the handler is restored, causing it to be processed by the wrong handler if another thread has already swapped in a different one. `XSync()` helps force pending errors to be processed, but without a mutex to serialize all handler changes and X11 activity, the race remains.

<details>
<summary>💡 Suggested guard</summary>

```diff
-                X_BAD_WINDOW_DETECTED.store(false, Ordering::SeqCst);
-                let prev_handler = XSetErrorHandler(Some(handle_x_error));
+                let _x11_handler_guard = X11_ERROR_HANDLER_LOCK
+                    .lock()
+                    .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
+                X_BAD_WINDOW_DETECTED.store(false, Ordering::SeqCst);
+                let prev_handler = XSetErrorHandler(Some(handle_x_error));
```

```rust
lazy_static::lazy_static! {
    static ref X11_ERROR_HANDLER_LOCK: std::sync::Mutex<()> = std::sync::Mutex::new(());
}
```
</details>

<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

handler swap and subsequent calls to libxdo_sys::xdo_get_window_location,
libxdo_sys::xdo_get_window_size, DISPLAY.with(...)/XSync, and the
XSetErrorHandler(prev_handler) restore inside a process-wide mutex (e.g. a
lazy_static Mutex named X11_ERROR_HANDLER_LOCK) to serialize all
XSetErrorHandler changes; specifically, acquire the lock before calling
XSetErrorHandler(Some(handle_x_error)), perform the location/size calls and the
DISPLAY.with(...) XSync flush while holding the lock, then call
XSetErrorHandler(prev_handler) and release the lock, ensuring
X_BAD_WINDOW_DETECTED is reliably observed and no handler-swapping races occur.
```

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>

🌐 Web query:

`Does libxdo's xdo_new open and use its own Display connection for xdo_get_window_location/xdo_get_window_size, and does XSync only flush requests/errors for the Display* passed to it?`

💡 Result:

- **Yes.** `xdo_new(const char *display_name)` calls `XOpenDisplay(display_name)` and then builds the `xdo_t` around that `Display*` (`xdo->xdpy`). ([sources.debian.org](https://sources.debian.org/src/xdotool/1%3A3.20160805.1-3/xdo.c))  
  Consequently, `xdo_get_window_location` and `xdo_get_window_size` use **that same** `Display*` (`xdo->xdpy`) when they call `XGetWindowAttributes`, `XQueryTree`, and `XTranslateCoordinates`. ([sources.debian.org](https://sources.debian.org/src/xdotool/1%3A3.20160805.1-3/xdo.c))  
  (If you don’t want libxdo to open its own connection, use `xdo_new_with_opened_display(Display *xdpy, ...)`.) ([sources.debian.org](https://sources.debian.org/src/xdotool/1%3A3.20160805.1-3/xdo.c))  

- **Yes.** `XSync(Display *display, ...)` flushes and synchronizes **only the output buffer/requests for the `Display*` you pass**, and it processes errors received by Xlib for that connection. It does not flush/synchronize other `Display*` connections you may have open. ([x.org](https://www.x.org/releases/X11R7.5/doc/man/man3/XSync.3.html?utm_source=openai))

**Sync the libxdo connection, not the separate thread-local `DISPLAY`.**

The `xdo_t` created by `libxdo_sys::xdo_new()` opens and uses its own `Display*` connection, but this code issues `XSync` on a different connection (`DISPLAY`). Since `XSync` only flushes requests and processes errors for the specific `Display*` passed to it, a `BadWindow` error from libxdo will not be delivered before the error handler is restored, leaving the guard incomplete.

The opaque `xdo_t` structure does not expose a public getter for its `Display*`, and there is no public function in libxdo_sys to retrieve it. To fix this, either:
1. Pass the same connection to libxdo using `xdo_new_with_opened_display()` instead of `xdo_new()`, or
2. Find an alternative way to synchronize the correct connection.

```
errors from xdo_t won't be delivered; modify creation and use of the xdo_t to
ensure the same Display is synchronized: open or obtain the Display once, pass
that Display* into libxdo via xdo_new_with_opened_display() instead of
xdo_new(), store and use that same Display for XSync (instead of DISPLAY) when
flushing before restoring the error handler, ensuring the XSync targets the
Display used by the xdo_t.
```

body: ## Pull request overview Fixes an X11 `BadWindow` crash in the Linux `get_focused_display()` path that can disconnect sessions when a focused window is destroyed mid-query. **Changes:** - Add a custom X11 error handler to suppress `BadWindow` errors during active-window geometry queries. - Treat X11/libxdo geometry-query failures as a graceful “no focused display” result instead of crashing. - Minor log formatting cleanup for GNOME shortcuts inhibitor permission handling. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`XSetErrorHandler` is process-global, but the detection flags (`X_BAD_WINDOW_DETECTED` / `X_UNEXPECTED_ERROR_DETECTED`) are also process-global. This means X11 errors from *other* threads during this window can flip these flags and make `get_focused_display` spuriously fail (and can also mask/redirect those errors). This repo does run other X11/libxdo calls on other threads (e.g., `get_cursor_pos()` is used from a dedicated thread in `src/server/input_service.rs`), so this is not purely hypothetical.

Consider making the detection state thread-local (e.g., `thread_local!` `Cell<bool>`), and/or track the target `window` in thread-local storage so the handler only treats `BadWindow` for the window being queried as a “hit”. If you want to avoid other threads being affected at all, add a global mutex and ensure all X11/libxdo calls that might run concurrently also take it while this temporary handler is installed.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: PR description says the fix uses `XSync` to flush deferred X11 errors before restoring the previous error handler, but the implementation explicitly avoids calling `XSync` and relies on the libxdo queries being synchronous. Either update the PR description to match the actual behavior, or (if flushing is required for correctness) implement the sync on the same `Display*` that libxdo uses (not the separate `DISPLAY` connection).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer
copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Forward non-BadWindow errors to previous X handler**

`XSetErrorHandler` is process-global, so while `get_focused_display()` installs `handle_x_error`, X11 errors from other threads/displays can also land here. In the non-BadWindow path this handler just sets a flag and returns `0`, which suppresses the previous handler for those unrelated errors. That changes global error semantics and can let unrelated X11 faults continue silently instead of being handled by the original handler, making cross-thread X failures hard to detect and potentially unsafe.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

This is a trade-off, mainly to prevent crashes.

The scope of XSetErrorHandler has been minimized as much as possible.

body:
## Pull request overview This PR adds a built-in option (`hide-stop-service`) to hide UI controls that stop/toggle the service while the service is running across desktop UI, tray menu, and Android UI. **Changes:** - Add `hide-stop-service` option checks in desktop UI (Sciter + Flutter), tray menu, and Android UI (server page + floating window). - Add an Android JNI/FFI binding to read built-in options (`getBuildinOption`) from Kotlin/Dart. - Temporarily update the `hbb_common` submodule and `.gitmodules` to a fork/branch that includes the needed key. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui/index.tis | Conditionally hides the “Enable service” menu item when configured and service is running. | | src/tray.rs | Conditionally removes “Stop service” tray menu item when `hide-stop-service=Y`. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Adds JNI function to read a built-in option key from Android. | | libs/hbb_common | Updates submodule commit to version containing the new option key. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Hides “Stop service” (and screen-capture toggle row while running) based on `hide-stop-service`. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Hides the desktop “Service” card while running when configured. | | flutter/lib/consts.dart | Adds Dart constant for the new option key. | | flutter/android/app/src/main/kotlin/ffi.kt | Adds Kotlin native binding to retrieve built-in option values. | | flutter/android/app/src/main/kotlin/com/carriez/flutter_hbb/FloatingWindowService.kt | Hides floating menu “Stop service” entry when configured. | | .gitmodules | Points `hbb_common` submodule at a fork + branch (temporary per PR description). |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Pointing a core dependency submodule to a personal fork + branch is a supply-chain risk and can make builds non-reproducible over time. Prefer keeping the submodule URL on the official upstream (or an org-controlled fork) and pinning only the commit in the superproject; if a temporary patch is needed, consider a short-lived commit on an org-owned fork or carrying the minimal patch locally until upstream merges.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The comment references a `stop-service` option, but the code is checking `hide-stop-service`. Updating the comment to match the actual option name will avoid confusion when maintaining this logic.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `getBuildinOption` appears to be a misspelling of `getBuiltinOption`. Since this is part of the Kotlin<->JNI API surface (and referenced from Dart bindings), consider introducing the correctly spelled name and keeping `getBuildinOption` as a backwards-compatible alias (or renaming consistently across Rust JNI + Kotlin + Dart before release) to avoid locking in a typo in the public interface.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This performs an FFI option lookup during every widget build. If the built-in option is effectively static for the session, cache it (e.g., read once in `initState`, store in the model, or memoize via a state variable) to avoid repeated platform/FFI calls during rebuilds.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: As with the mobile page, reading a built-in option via FFI in `build()` can be unnecessarily costly on frequent rebuilds. If this option doesn’t change at runtime, cache it outside the build path (initState/controller/model) to reduce overhead and keep UI rebuilds cheap.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The option key is duplicated as a raw string here while other layers use constants (e.g., Dart `kOptionHideStopService`). To reduce drift/typos, consider defining a Kotlin constant (e.g., in `FFI` or a shared constants file) and referencing it here.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview Adds a new built-in option `hide-stop-service` that hides stop/toggle service controls across desktop UI, tray menu, and Android UI while the service is running. **Changes:** - Add `hide-stop-service` handling to desktop Sciter UI and desktop Flutter settings UI. - Hide “Stop service” entry in the tray menu and Android (server page + floating window menu) when the option is enabled. - Update `hbb_common` submodule to include the new option key. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui/index.tis | Conditionally hides the service toggle entry when service is running and option is enabled. | | src/tray.rs | Conditionally omits the “Stop service” tray menu item based on built-in option. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Exposes a JNI getter for built-in options to Android (for Kotlin-side usage). | | libs/hbb_common | Advances submodule commit to pick up new option key constant(s). | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Hides stop/toggle UI on Android server page when option is enabled and service is running. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Hides Stop button in desktop settings while service is running when option is enabled. | | flutter/lib/consts.dart | Adds Dart constant for the new built-in option key. | | flutter/android/app/src/main/kotlin/ffi.kt | Declares JNI binding for built-in option getter. | | flutter/android/app/src/main/kotlin/com/carriez/flutter_hbb/FloatingWindowService.kt | Hides floating-window “Stop service” menu item via built-in option. | | .gitmodules | Points `hbb_common` submodule to a fork/branch to supply the needed patch. |

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).

body: Pointing a submodule URL to a personal fork/branch is brittle for downstream consumers (availability, trust, mirroring, and long-term maintenance). If the upstream `rustdesk/hbb_common` doesn’t yet contain the required commit, prefer hosting the temporary branch under an official org-controlled fork (or land the change upstream first) rather than changing the canonical submodule URL to a personal namespace.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This comment mentions the `stop-service` option, but the logic added here is for `hide-stop-service`. Please update the comment to reference the correct option name (or rephrase to avoid naming a different option), so future readers don’t misinterpret the behavior.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This reads a native/binding value during `build()`, which will re-run on every rebuild. Since this is a built-in option (typically static for the app session), consider caching it in state (e.g., in `initState`) or storing it in a reactive model so rebuilds don’t repeatedly cross the FFI/binding boundary.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Same concern as desktop: calling `bind.mainGetBuildinOption(...)` directly in `build()` can cause repeated native/binding calls during frequent rebuilds. If the value is not expected to change at runtime, cache it in state or a model layer and read the cached value here.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The exported JNI symbol name uses `getBuildinOption` (missing the second 't' in 'Builtin'). Since this becomes part of the Android native API surface, consider introducing a correctly spelled alias (e.g., `getBuiltinOption`) while keeping `getBuildinOption` for backward compatibility, to reduce long-term confusion in Kotlin/Java bindings.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The option key is hard-coded as a string literal here. To avoid drift/typos across platforms, consider centralizing it as a Kotlin constant (or reusing an existing shared constant if you have one on Android) and referencing the constant instead of repeating the raw key.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The event handling branches duplicate the `open_i` handling depending on whether `quit_i` exists. Consider restructuring to handle `open_i` first (or use an `Option<id>` comparison) so the logic stays single-path and easier to extend when adding more tray items.

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`Does Flutter 3.24.5 stable already include DropdownMenu enableFilter support, or is a downstream patch still required?`

💡 Result:

Yes, Flutter 3.24.5 stable includes DropdownMenu enableFilter support natively. The enableFilter property has been part of DropdownMenu since its introduction in late 2022 (PR `#116088`), with a default filter implementation. The filterCallback enhancement (PR `#143939`, merged June 3, 2024) provides custom filtering and is also included, as Flutter 3.24.0 was released August 6, 2024, and 3.24.5 (November 13, 2024) is a later hotfix release on the stable channel. Official API docs confirm both enableFilter (defaults to false) and optional filterCallback are available, with usage examples. Recent issues and Stack Overflow posts from late 2024 discuss using enableFilter in stable contexts without mentioning patches needed. No evidence of requiring downstream patches; it's fully supported in official stable releases.

Citations:

- 1: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/143939
- 2: https://docs.flutter.dev/release/release-notes/release-notes-3.24.0

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reset client dedupe state after remote clipboard writes**

`should_send_msg` permanently suppresses any clipboard payload that matches the last one this side sent, but that cache is never invalidated when the local clipboard is overwritten by an incoming remote update (`update_clipboard(..., ClipboardSide::Client)` in the receive path). Because owner-marked remote writes are filtered out of `check_clipboard`, the cache can stay at an old value (e.g., `A`), and a later user re-copy of `A` is dropped even if the peer currently has `B`, causing clipboard desynchronization.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
author: chatgpt-codex-connector

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reset server dedupe state after remote clipboard writes**

The server-side dedupe cache has the same lifetime problem: `last_clipboard_sig` is only updated on sends and never reset when host clipboard content is changed by an incoming client message (`update_clipboard(..., ClipboardSide::Host)` path). Since owner-tagged writes are excluded by `check_clipboard`, re-copying a previously sent value can be suppressed indefinitely, so other clients may not receive the host’s latest intended clipboard state.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
author: chatgpt-codex-connector

body: There is no need to check `if handler.should_send_clipboard_msg(&msg)` here , because `CallbackResult::Next` indicates clipboard changed.
author: fufesou

body:
Why do we need to send clipboard updates from the `RecvTimeoutError::Timeout` branch?

A timeout only means no callback was received; it does not necessarily mean the clipboard changed.
Master only sends updates on `CallbackResult::Next`, so this turns the Wayland path into polling.

Could you please comment on this block if there is a specific reason this fallback is needed?
author: fufesou

body: Please also comment on this block if there is a specific reason for `handler.check_clipboard()`.

body:
## Pull request overview This PR refactors permanent (“master”) password handling to store a non-reversible hashed verifier and ensures UI/IPC no longer exposes or pre-fills secret material, mitigating local recovery risk from synced configs. **Changes:** - Introduces hashed permanent-password storage semantics and verification logic (including constant-time comparison). - Updates IPC/UI/Flutter/Sciter flows to query “is set” state and use explicit ACK/NACK on password updates rather than reading secrets back. - Adds syncing of verifier storage from daemon to user config (desktop) without exposing plaintext. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Replaces password readback with “is set” query and blocks changes when disabled. | | src/ui/index.tis | Removes pre-filling permanent password in the Sciter dialog. | | src/ui.rs | Removes Sciter handler method that returned permanent password. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds hashed-storage verification path + constant-time comparison. | | src/ipc.rs | Adds IPC endpoints for “set”/preset state and verifier syncing; introduces ACK/NACK on writes. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Updates Flutter FFI to query “is set” and set password with success result. | | libs/hbb_common | Updates submodule to required config changes. | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Updates web bridge API surface for new FFI method. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Removes logic that verified success via secret readback. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Removes dialog that pre-filled existing permanent password. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Switches from secret readback to “is set” boolean query. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Switches from secret readback to “is set” boolean query. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Removes pre-fill and uses new ACK-based API when setting permanent password. | | flutter/lib/common.dart | Updates URL-handler flow to display success/failure based on ACK result. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This condition appears inverted vs the previous behavior. Previously it opened the flow when the permanent password was not set (`mainGetPermanentPassword().isEmpty`). With the new boolean API, it likely should trigger when the password is NOT set (i.e., negate the boolean), otherwise the UI may prompt only when a permanent password already exists.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Same inversion as on mobile: the old logic triggered when the permanent password was missing. With `mainIsPermanentPasswordSet()` returning a bool, this likely needs negation to preserve the intended UX.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `get_config` is defined (in the provided context) as an async function, but it’s being called from a synchronous function without awaiting. This will not compile (or may call a different symbol than intended). Consider making `is_permanent_password_set` async and providing a separate blocking wrapper (similar to the existing tokio-driven wrappers), or call `get_config_async` via a runtime/handle explicitly. Also, the comments mention retrying/looping, but there is no loop or retry implemented in this function as written.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using `#[tokio::main]` on a library-style function can create a new runtime per call and may panic if invoked from within an existing Tokio runtime (nested runtime). Prefer exposing an async API that runs on the app’s existing runtime, or use a shared runtime/`Handle::try_current()` (falling back to a lazily-initialized runtime) to avoid per-call runtime construction and potential reentrancy issues.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Pull request overview Refactors permanent (“master”) password handling so UIs/IPC no longer read or pre-fill secrets, aligning with the new non-reversible verifier storage format. **Changes:** - Replace “get permanent password” flows with boolean “is set” / “is preset” queries and storage-sync from daemon. - Harden server-side verification with hashed-storage decoding + constant-time comparison. - Update Flutter/Sciter UIs to stop pre-filling permanent password and to handle explicit ACK/NACK on writes. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Exposes `is_permanent_password_set()` and blocks local changes when disabled; desktop syncs storage from daemon. | | src/ui/index.tis | Removes permanent password pre-fill in Sciter dialog. | | src/ui.rs | Removes Sciter handler for reading permanent password. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds verifier-based validation path + constant-time comparison. | | src/ipc.rs | Adds IPC endpoints for “set state”, “preset state”, and storage+salt sync; adds ACK/NACK for writes. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Switches Flutter API from reading secret to boolean state and write-with-result. | | libs/hbb_common | Updates submodule dependency for config/verifier support. | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Renames web bridge method to new “with result” API signature. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Removes old “set and read back to verify” permanent password setter. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Removes mobile permanent password dialog that pre-filled/read secrets. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Uses `mainIsPermanentPasswordSet()` instead of reading password string. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Uses `mainIsPermanentPasswordSet()` instead of reading password string. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Stop pre-fill; handle ACK/NACK and show failure. | | flutter/lib/common.dart | URL-triggered permanent password set now uses result boolean. |

body: The IPC payload format `storage + "\n" + salt` is delimiter-fragile (any newline in either field breaks parsing) and also makes forward-compat changes harder. Prefer a structured encoding (e.g., JSON via `serde_json`, or a length-prefixed/binary encoding) so parsing is unambiguous and extensible.

A more robust pattern is to expose an async `*_async` API for async callers and provide a dedicated blocking wrapper that uses an existing runtime handle when present (or a shared/global runtime) rather than instantiating a new runtime each time.

## Pull request overview Refactors the permanent (“master”) password flow to avoid exposing plaintext in UI/IPC, support hashed verifier storage, and improve validation semantics across daemon/UI/Flutter. **Changes:** - Replaces UI/IPC “read back password” flows with “is set” / storage-sync semantics. - Updates server-side password verification to handle hashed storage formats and uses constant-time comparison. - Updates Flutter/UI integrations to avoid pre-filling secrets and to handle success/failure via explicit results. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Replaces password getter with boolean “is set” query + optional daemon->user storage sync; blocks changes when disabled. | | src/ui/index.tis | Stops pre-filling permanent password fields in the Sciter dialog. | | src/ui.rs | Removes Sciter-exposed permanent password getter from UI bindings. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds verifier-based validation paths and constant-time comparison for password checks. | | src/ipc.rs | Adds IPC endpoints for “is set”, “is preset”, and storage+salt sync; adds ACK/NACK semantics for setting permanent password. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Replaces plaintext getter/setter with boolean “is set” and “set with result” functions; updates preset detection. | | libs/hbb_common | Bumps submodule for new config/verifier support. | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Updates web bridge method signature for the new “set with result” API. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Removes readback-based permanent password success check helper. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Removes mobile permanent password dialog that pre-filled and relied on reading back secrets. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Switches “is permanent password set” gates to boolean query. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Switches “is permanent password set” gates to boolean query. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Stops pre-filling password fields; shows failure when daemon rejects; minor formatting changes. | | flutter/lib/common.dart | Uses “set with result” and shows toast based on boolean success. |

Fix by either (a) making these functions async and awaiting `get_config(...)`, (b) calling the existing `get_config_async(...)` via a single shared runtime/handle, or (c) providing a dedicated synchronous wrapper function (that does not create nested runtimes—see next comment) and using that consistently.

body: Using `#[tokio::main]` on a non-`main` function creates a new runtime per call and can panic when invoked from within an existing Tokio runtime (“Cannot start a runtime from within a runtime”), which is a realistic scenario in GUI/FFI codepaths. Prefer exposing an `async fn` API and letting callers `.await` it, or provide a synchronous wrapper that uses `tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()` (when available) and otherwise reuses a lazily-initialized runtime (not one per call).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Packing `(storage, salt)` into a single string with `"\n"` as a separator is brittle and couples the protocol to an implicit “no newlines” constraint. A more robust approach is to return two independent config keys, introduce a dedicated IPC message/struct/tuple payload, or encode as a structured format (e.g., JSON) so parsing is unambiguous and forward-compatible.

body:
## Pull request overview Refactors permanent (“master”) password handling to avoid exposing or synchronizing recoverable secrets, moving toward non-reversible verifier storage and “is set” semantics across UI/IPC/Flutter. **Changes:** - Replace permanent-password readback with boolean “is set” and hashed-storage sync from daemon. - Harden server-side verification flow (hashed storage support + constant-time comparison). - Update Sciter + Flutter UIs to stop pre-filling password fields and to use ACK/NACK for set operations. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Adds `is_permanent_password_set()` and blocks password changes when policy disables it. | | src/ui/index.tis | Removes pre-fill of existing permanent password in Sciter dialog. | | src/ui.rs | Removes Sciter handler for reading permanent password. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds hashed-storage verification path + constant-time comparison helper; adjusts validation flow. | | src/ipc.rs | Introduces IPC endpoints for “is set”, “is preset”, and syncing hashed storage; adds ACK/NACK on password set. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Switches Flutter API from “get password” to “is set” + “set with result”. | | libs/hbb_common | Updates submodule to version that supports new hashed permanent password storage APIs. | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Updates web bridge method signature for new “set with result” API. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Removes legacy “set then readback to confirm” permanent password flow. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Removes mobile dialog that pre-filled permanent password via readback. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Uses `mainIsPermanentPasswordSet` instead of `mainGetPermanentPassword().isEmpty`. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Same “is set” swap for desktop settings. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Stops pre-filling password dialog and checks set result via boolean ACK. | | flutter/lib/common.dart | Uses boolean result for setting password from URL handler. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `get_config` is defined as an `async fn` in the provided context excerpt, but it’s being called here as if it were synchronous (using `?` and pattern matching directly). This won’t compile unless there is a separate synchronous `get_config` wrapper in scope. Fix by either (a) making `sync_permanent_password_storage_from_daemon` async and awaiting `get_config`, or (b) introducing a synchronous wrapper (similar to `set_permanent_password_with_ack`) that blocks on `get_config_async`."
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Same issue as above: these functions call `get_config(...)` as if it were synchronous, but `get_config` is shown as async in the context excerpt. Convert these functions to async + `await`, or add/ensure a dedicated synchronous wrapper around `get_config_async` and use that here.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: On desktop, local hashed storage is only synced when the daemon reports the password is set. If the daemon clears the permanent password, this path returns `false` without syncing, leaving stale user-side storage behind. Since `sync_permanent_password_storage_from_daemon()` already handles empty storage by clearing, call it unconditionally (or also call it in the `else` branch) to keep local config consistent with the daemon.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This `if ... else` makes the hard/preset password path unreachable whenever `local_storage` is non-empty. If `HARD_SETTINGS.password` is meant to be accepted regardless of local storage (e.g., enforced/preset deployments), this is a behavior change from validating both sources. Consider validating the hard password in addition to local storage (separate `if` blocks), or document and enforce precedence explicitly (e.g., hard overrides local).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using `#[tokio::main]` on a public function inside a library/module can panic if it’s ever called from within an existing Tokio runtime (\"Cannot start a runtime from within a runtime\"). Prefer exposing an `async fn` API and providing a small runtime-aware blocking wrapper (e.g., use `tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()` + `block_in_place`/`Handle::block_on`, otherwise create a runtime) for synchronous call sites.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Manual constant-time comparison is a good improvement, but Rust/LLVM does not strictly guarantee constant-time behavior under optimization. For cryptographic comparisons, consider using a well-reviewed constant-time implementation (e.g., `subtle::ConstantTimeEq`) to reduce the risk of compiler/CPU introducing data-dependent behavior. This would also simplify the code.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Pull request overview Refactors permanent (“master”) password handling to avoid exposing or storing reversible secrets by shifting desktop/UI/IPC to “is set” + hashed-verifier storage, while keeping backward compatibility for legacy values. **Changes:** - Replace plaintext readback flows with boolean “is set” semantics across UI/IPC/Flutter. - Add daemon ↔ user config synchronization of hashed permanent-password storage (storage + salt), plus daemon ACK/NACK on password writes. - Harden server-side password verification with separate handling for hashed storage vs legacy plaintext inputs and constant-time comparison. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Switch UI API from returning permanent password to boolean “is set” + daemon sync behavior | | src/ui/index.tis | Remove pre-fill of permanent password fields in Sciter dialog | | src/ui.rs | Remove Sciter handler method that exposed permanent password | | src/server/connection.rs | Add hashed-storage verification path + constant-time compare + clearer separation of “plain” vs “storage” validation | | src/ipc.rs | Add IPC endpoints for permanent-password storage/salt + “is set”/“is preset”, plus write ACK/NACK and sync helpers | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Add FFI “is set” + set-with-result APIs; avoid secret readback for preset detection | | libs/hbb_common | Update submodule to version that supports new permanent-password storage/decoding | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Update web bridge signature to new set-with-result API | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Remove old “set then readback” permanent-password verification flow | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Remove dialog implementation that pre-filled/read back permanent password | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Switch “needs permanent password” logic to boolean “is set” | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Switch “needs permanent password” logic to boolean “is set” | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Stop pre-filling password UI; handle daemon ACK/NACK result | | flutter/lib/common.dart | Update URL scheme handler to use set-with-result and show success/failure |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `#[tokio::main]` on a public library function can panic when called from within an existing Tokio runtime (common in GUI/event-loop or async contexts): it tries to create a new runtime. Prefer exposing only an `async fn` and having callers `await` it, or provide a sync wrapper that uses `tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()` to `block_on` when already inside a runtime (and otherwise create a runtime explicitly).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `local_storage_is_empty` influences whether a sync is attempted, but the function always returns `daemon_is_set`. If local storage is non-empty while the daemon reports unset (or is unreachable and returns false), callers may incorrectly treat the permanent password as “not set” even though local verifier storage exists. If the intent is “set if either daemon or local storage indicates it”, return `daemon_is_set || !local_storage_is_empty`; if the intent is “daemon authoritative”, consider removing the local check (or renaming variables / adding a comment) to avoid misleading logic.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The sync and async variants duplicate the same parsing and application logic. Consider factoring out a shared helper that takes the payload string (or `Option<String>`) and performs split/validation/application once. This reduces the chance of future drift (e.g., changing delimiter/format handling in one path but not the other).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This swallows the error context and only returns `false`, which makes troubleshooting daemon/IPC failures harder (especially when UI only shows a generic 'Failed'). Consider logging the error (at least at warn level) before returning `false`, or returning a richer result over FFI if feasible.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: If UI polls this method (e.g., on timers or frequent state refreshes), the unconditional `warn` on any failure can generate noisy logs (especially when the daemon is intentionally absent in portable scenarios). Consider lowering to `debug`, rate-limiting, or logging only when failure transitions from success → failure.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Pull request overview This PR refactors “permanent password” handling to prevent recoverable secrets from being exposed via local config and service/user config synchronization, switching to a non-reversible verifier and updating UI/IPC/FFI to only expose “is set” semantics. **Changes:** - Store permanent password as a hashed verifier and migrate legacy stored values while keeping verification backward compatible. - Update IPC/UI/FFI to avoid returning or pre-filling the permanent password, returning only boolean state and ACK/NACK on updates. - Add constant-time password verifier comparison and adjust server-side validation paths for storage vs plaintext presets. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Replaces password readback with “is set” query and daemon-to-user sync of verifier state. | | src/ui/index.tis | Removes pre-fill of permanent password in Sciter dialog. | | src/ui.rs | Removes Sciter binding for reading the permanent password. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds constant-time verify, supports hashed storage decoding, and separates preset/plain validation. | | src/ipc.rs | Adds IPC endpoints for “is set”, storage+salt sync, and ACK/NACK for password updates. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Switches Flutter FFI to boolean “is set” and result-bearing set call. | | libs/hbb_common | Updates submodule to version containing new config/verifier primitives. | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Updates web bridge API to new “set with result” function signature. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Removes old “set permanent password then read back” verification flow. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Removes mobile dialog that pre-filled the permanent password. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Uses “is set” boolean instead of reading permanent password. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Uses “is set” boolean instead of reading permanent password. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Removes pre-fill and uses returned result to show failure without readback. | | flutter/lib/common.dart | Updates URL-triggered password set flow to respect success/failure result. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `#[tokio::main]` on a library/helper function can panic when called from within an existing Tokio runtime (\"Cannot start a runtime from within a runtime\"), which is a realistic scenario for IPC helpers in GUI apps or when called from async contexts. Prefer exposing only the async `*_async` API and having callers `await` it, or implement a sync wrapper that uses `tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()` and `block_on` (and only builds a new runtime when no handle exists).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This logs a warning for any timeout/connection failure and returns `false`, which can lead to log spam if the UI polls this value (and can also mislead UI into thinking the password is unset whenever the daemon is unreachable). Consider returning `ResultType<bool>` (or `Option<bool>`) so callers can distinguish \"unset\" vs \"unknown\", and/or rate-limit or downgrade the log level for expected transient failures.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Encoding `storage` and `salt` as a newline-delimited payload is brittle (it silently relies on neither field ever containing `\\n`, and makes future extensions difficult). Prefer a structured encoding (e.g., JSON, bincode, or length-prefixed fields) so the payload format is unambiguous and extensible.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Manually implementing constant-time equality is easy to get subtly wrong across platforms/optimizations and is tricky to audit over time. Consider using a well-reviewed constant-time comparison utility (e.g., `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` or an equivalent vetted helper already in your dependency tree) to reduce risk and make the intent clearer.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: On desktop this can trigger up to two IPC round-trips (`is_permanent_password_set` + potentially a sync) every time the UI asks for the state. If this function is called frequently (e.g., during widget rebuilds/polling), it can add noticeable IPC overhead. Consider caching the daemon state (short TTL) and only syncing when the daemon state transitions or when you detect local stale storage once per session.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

This call introduces a compile-time break for web builds: `bind` resolves to `RustdeskImpl` from `flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart` via the conditional import in `platform_model.dart`, but `RustdeskImpl` does not define `mainIsPermanentPasswordSet`. Because `DesktopSettingPage` is used by web settings (`flutter/lib/web/settings_page.dart`), the new call cannot be resolved on web and the target fails to compile until a matching web-bridge method is added.

This function is called from UI code (e.g., menu rendering/update in `index.tis`), so it can introduce UI stalls and repeated IPC load. Consider separating “sync” from “check” (e.g., sync once when opening the password menu/dialog, then read local state thereafter), caching the result with a short TTL, or making the sync path explicitly asynchronous/non-blocking for UI usage. **src/ui/index.tis:1** * Returning a single-space string as a validation error will block submission but provides no visible/meaningful feedback, which can be confusing (especially if submit is triggered via Enter/programmatic paths). Prefer either returning a real translated message (e.g., “Please enter a password”), or treating this case as “no-op” (return `null`/success and close without changes) while keeping the submit button disabled to prevent accidental submits.

If the intent is to depend on upstream PR rustdesk/hbb_common#507, prefer keeping the submodule URL on the upstream repo and pinning to a specific merged commit; avoid `branch = ...` unless there is a strong operational reason and you have controls to ensure immutability.

body:
## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 65 out of 65 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (4) **src/server/connection.rs:1** * Falling back to treating an *intended hashed storage value* as plaintext when decoding fails can catastrophically weaken auth if the stored value is corrupted/malformed but begins with the `01` prefix (e.g., `storage = \"01\"` becomes a trivially guessable plaintext password). Safer behavior: if the storage indicates the hashed format (prefix `01`), require successful decode/length validation and return `false` on decode failure; only use plaintext fallback when the value does not indicate hashed format. **src/ui_interface.rs:1** * On desktop, `is_permanent_password_set()` can trigger an IPC connect + sync on every call (and this function is used from UI render paths). This can add noticeable latency and load. Consider removing the implicit sync from this getter and doing sync only (a) once on startup, (b) after a successful set/clear, or (c) when a cheap local check shows the local storage state is out of date (e.g., if daemon says set and local storage is empty). **src/server/connection.rs:1** * For cryptographic comparisons it’s safer to use a vetted constant-time equality implementation (`subtle::ConstantTimeEq`, `ring::constant_time`, etc.). Hand-rolled loops can be subject to compiler optimizations or micro-architectural differences that are easy to miss. If adding a dependency is undesirable, consider using an existing constant-time primitive already present in the dependency graph. **src/ui/index.tis:1** * Returning a single space as a validation error is a brittle UI hack (it can render as an empty error, affect layout, and is unclear to future maintainers). Prefer a clear validation result: either return a meaningful message (if empty inputs should be rejected), or return a successful validation result and rely on the disabled submit button logic to prevent submission when both fields are empty.

## Pull request overview Refactors “permanent/master password” handling to avoid reversible local storage and to prevent UI/IPC from exposing secret material, using a non-reversible hashed verifier with backward-compatible migration. **Changes:** - Replaces UI/IPC “readback” of permanent password with “is set” semantics and storage/salt sync from the daemon. - Updates server-side password validation to accept either hashed storage (`01...`) or legacy plaintext-like values. - Adjusts desktop (Sciter) and Flutter UI flows to avoid pre-filling secrets and to support clearing the permanent password. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 64 out of 64 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/ui_interface.rs | Replaces password readback with boolean “is set” queries and daemon→user storage sync. | | src/ui/msgbox.tis | Prevents submit event when the submit button is disabled. | | src/ui/index.tis | Removes password pre-fill, adds “clear password” UI, and disables submit when empty. | | src/ui/common.css | Adds styling for disabled buttons. | | src/ui.rs | Updates Sciter UI bridge to expose “is set” functions and uses ACK-based setter internally. | | src/server/connection.rs | Adds hashed-storage verification path with constant-time comparison. | | src/ipc.rs | Adds IPC endpoints for “is set”, “is preset”, and storage+salt sync; adds ACK/NACK for writes. | | src/flutter_ffi.rs | Replaces permanent password getters with an ACK-based setter; avoids exposing secrets. | | libs/hbb_common | Updates submodule to include config/hash storage support. | | flutter/lib/web/bridge.dart | Updates web bridge API surface to match new permanent-password setter signature. | | flutter/lib/models/server_model.dart | Removes old “set then readback” permanent password success detection. | | flutter/lib/mobile/widgets/dialog.dart | Removes dialog that pre-filled permanent password from readback. | | flutter/lib/mobile/pages/server_page.dart | Switches “is set” check from password readback to common key state. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart | Switches “is set” check from password readback to common key state. | | flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_home_page.dart | Removes pre-fill, adds “remove” flow, and uses ACK-based setter. | | flutter/lib/common.dart | Uses ACK-based setter and shows success/failure based on ACK. | | src/lang/en.rs | Adds new UI tip
